services.title: External Attack Surface Assessment
services.tagline: See what attackers see before they do
services.description: Comprehensive external reconnaissance that maps your entire digital footprint. We identify exposed services, misconfigurations, leaked credentials, and vulnerable entry points — all from an attacker's perspective, without touching your internal network.

services.title: Web Application Security Audit
services.description: Manual security assessment of web applications covering OWASP Top 10, business logic flaws, API security, and authentication flows. Hypothesis-driven testing that finds what scanners miss.

services.title: Threat Intelligence
services.tagline: Know your threats before they strike
services.description: Proactive threat hunting, phishing campaign analysis, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, and actionable intelligence reporting for organizations facing targeted threats.

services.title: Continuous Attack Surface Monitoring
services.description: Automated monitoring that alerts you when new subdomains appear, services are exposed, SSL certificates expire, or credentials leak. Monthly reports with trend analysis.

cta.heading: Ready to see your attack surface?
cta.subheading: Start with a free 15-minute consultation to discuss your security needs.

faq.q: Do you need access to our internal systems?
faq.a: No. Our External Attack Surface Assessment is performed entirely from the outside, just like a real attacker would. No VPN, no credentials, no internal access needed.

faq.q: Is this legal? Will it disrupt our services?
faq.a: All testing is performed with your written authorization. External assessments use passive and low-impact techniques that won't affect your service availability.

faq.q: What do we get at the end?
faq.a: A comprehensive report with an executive summary, detailed technical findings, asset inventory, and a prioritized remediation checklist. Plus a 30-minute debrief call to walk through the results.

faq.q: How is this different from a vulnerability scanner?
faq.a: Scanners find known CVEs. We combine automated reconnaissance with manual analysis to find misconfigurations, leaked credentials, forgotten services, and architectural weaknesses that scanners miss.
